A team adopts Deutschian standards and judges ideas by whether their explanations are hard to vary. In that setting, early-stage hypotheses look weak, because they are often easy to vary. Yet the premises say many productive hypotheses begin exactly that way and become rigid only after mathematical consolidation. So the team cannot treat present flexibility as decisive evidence of bad explanation. If it does, it risks discarding the very material from which stronger theories later emerge.

Suppose someone replies that large language models fail creatively because genuine discovery would require searching an intractably vast space. The anomaly immediately appears: humans also do not consciously inspect vast search spaces. Therefore, the intractability framing cannot, by itself, cleanly separate human creativity from LLM failure. If intractability explains one, it threatens to explain the other. So the team learns that this line of argument is at best incomplete. Either both humans and LLMs rely on something other than explicit vast search, or the explanation has not yet located the relevant difference.

At this point they turn to a famous journal case, presented as a tidy sequence from anomaly to conceptual breakthrough. It is persuasive because it compresses decades of struggle into a smooth arc. But the premises identify this as suspicious. The retrospective neatness may be doing the work. If so, the example does not straightforwardly show how discovery actually happened; it may only show how history can be narrated after the fact. Narrative coherence is then being mistaken for explanatory discovery. The team must therefore discount the force of examples whose persuasive power comes from hindsight compression.

Meanwhile, the anxious secretary updated the clandestine femme timeline. That event fixes at least one concrete fact in the world: there exists a timeline, it concerns something clandestine and femme, and a secretary, being anxious, updated it. Separately, longing for the novel part, the handcuffed ball rolled away. So there is also a handcuffed ball whose rolling away occurred under that described condition.

These last two facts do not logically resolve the earlier methodological dispute. They simply coexist with it. The world implied by the premises is one in which theory assessment is under tension: strict hard-to-vary standards may undervalue fertile beginnings; appeals to intractable search do not uniquely explain machine limits because humans share the same apparent obstacle; and polished historical examples may owe their force to retrospective storytelling rather than genuine illumination of discovery. All the while, ordinary and peculiar events still occur: a secretary updates a secretive timeline, and a handcuffed ball rolls away while longing for the novel part.
